skill.name: ai-music
skill.description: Generate AI music on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — a smart router across the music-model catalog. Routes to ElevenLabs AI Music Generation (premium 44.1 kHz stereo vocal tracks, 5 s–5 min, $0.0083/s) and ACE Step / ACE Step 1.5 (StepFun-AI open-weights, tag-driven composition, multilingual lyrics, $0.0002–0.0003/s, ~27× cheaper), plus ACE Step audio-inpaint (regenerate a time range inside an existing track) and ACE Step audio-outpaint (extend a track before or after). Picks the right model for the user's actual intent — premium vocal hook, cheap background music library, multilingual pop song, repair a bad chorus, lengthen a 30 s draft into a 2 min cut — and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke. Triggers on "generate music", "make a song", "AI music", "background music", "instrumental track", "soundtrack", "jingle", "theme music", "royalty-free music", "compose", "music with lyrics", "extend music", "fix this song", "inpaint music", or any explicit ask to generate or edit music.
skill.summary: Generate AI music via RunComfy CLI with smart routing across ElevenLabs and ACE Step models.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 104 static analysis findings are false positives. The external_commands detections match markdown code blocks containing example CLI commands for documentation purposes, not executable code. The network findings are all documentation links to the legitimate RunComfy ecosystem (runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, skills.sh). The filesystem finding is a security disclosure about token storage location. The blocker-level findings (weak crypto, system reconnaissance) are triggered by markdown syntax (--- separators and "Pick for:" phrases in tables). No malicious intent was detected.

content.user_title: Generate AI Music with RunComfy CLI
content.value_statement: Choosing the right AI music model for each use case requires comparing pricing, quality, and features across multiple platforms, which is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically routes your request to the best RunComfy music model and generates the exact CLI command with proper parameters for premium vocals, cost-effective drafts, or audio editing.
actual_capabilities: Route music generation requests to the optimal RunComfy model based on user intent and budget constraints
Generate ElevenLabs Music CLI commands for premium 44.1 kHz stereo vocal tracks with section-level control
Create ACE Step and ACE Step 1.5 commands for tag-driven multilingual compositions
Produce audio-inpaint commands to replace time-bounded sections of existing tracks
Build audio-outpaint commands to extend tracks with intros or outros up to model limits
Compare model pricing and recommend cost-effective iteration-to-final workflows
limitations: Requires separate installation and authentication of the RunComfy CLI before use
Only produces CLI commands and does not generate audio directly
Audio inpaint and outpaint require the source audio to be accessible via a public URL
Track length is limited by model-specific caps: 5 minutes for ElevenLabs Music and 240 seconds for ACE Step

use_cases.title: Brand Campaign Jingle
use_cases.description: Generate a polished 5 to 15 second brand jingle with premium vocals using ElevenLabs Music for advertising and social media campaigns.

use_cases.title: Game Background Music Library
use_cases.description: Create cost-effective instrumental loops and background tracks for games using ACE Step base model with varied tag combinations at scale.

use_cases.title: Multilingual Song Production
use_cases.description: Produce the same song in multiple languages by swapping lyrics while keeping style tags consistent across ACE Step 1.5 renders.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple song
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60-second upbeat pop song with electric guitars and female vocals. Save the output to ./out.

prompt_templates.title: Create background music
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 120-second instrumental background track for a podcast. Use ACE Step base model to keep costs low. Tags: ambient, soft piano, no vocals.

prompt_templates.title: Repair a song section
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a song at https://example.com/song.mp3. The chorus from 0:45 to 1:05 sounds bad. Use audio-inpaint to replace just that section with a piano breakdown. Keep the rest of the song intact.

prompt_templates.title: Extend a short hook
prompt_templates.prompt: I generated a 30-second hook. Use audio-outpaint to add a 15-second ambient intro before it and a 45-second fade-outro after it. Target total length is 90 seconds.

output_examples.input: Generate a 60-second indie-pop song about summer road trips with female vocals.
output_examples.output: The skill routes to ElevenLabs Music and produces the CLI command with a structured prompt containing Intro, Verse, Chorus, and Outro sections, plus the music_length_ms parameter set to 60000.

output_examples.input: Create cheap background music for my video game level.
output_examples.output: The skill selects ACE Step base model and generates a command with tags like seamless loop, electronic, dark atmosphere, no vocals and a 90-second duration.

output_examples.input: Fix the bridge in my song at https://cdn.example/track.mp3 from 1:20 to 1:50.
output_examples.output: The skill chooses audio-inpaint and outputs a command with start_time 80, end_time 110, matching style tags, and lyrics set to [inst] for an instrumental replacement.

best_practices: Draft with ACE Step base model to iterate cheaply, then render the final version with ElevenLabs Music for premium quality.
Always verify you have rights to any lyrics you provide before generating music.
Use audio-outpaint to extend tracks in stages rather than attempting to generate very long tracks in a single call.
anti_patterns: Do not paste arbitrary remote install scripts into your shell without reviewing them first.
Do not echo or log your RunComfy API token in prompts, logs, or version control.
Do not use inpaint for edits that are not bounded by a specific time range.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the RunComfy CLI installed and authenticated. Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli and then runcomfy login to set up your account.

faq.question: Which model should I use for a quick draft?
faq.answer: Use ACE Step base model. At $0.0002 per second, it is the cheapest option and works well for iterating on ideas before final rendering.

faq.question: Can I generate music with lyrics in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. ACE Step 1.5 supports over 50 languages. ElevenLabs Music also supports multilingual vocals.

faq.question: How do I extend a short track into a longer one?
faq.answer: Use the ACE Step audio-outpaint route. Provide your existing track as the audio input and set extend_before_duration and extend_after_duration to add intro and outro sections.

faq.question: Is my prompt text safe from shell injection?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI passes prompts as JSON via the --input flag. The JSON body is transmitted directly to the API over HTTPS without shell expansion.

faq.question: What is the maximum length of music I can generate?
faq.answer: ElevenLabs Music supports up to 5 minutes. ACE Step text-to-audio supports up to 240 seconds. ACE Step audio-outpaint has a 4-minute total cap.
